steps.name: Define Your Niche
steps.console: Identify a focused topic with search demand and low competition. Narrow until the channel can own a specific corner of YouTube.

steps.name: Research Topics
steps.console: Use Perplexity AI to find the top 10 questions your audience searches. Filter for videos under 50K views — that is your opportunity window.

steps.name: Generate Your First Script
steps.console: Feed your topic into Claude with the hook–problem–solution–CTA structure. Target 1,500 words for a 10-minute video. Never start with 'Hey guys.'

steps.name: Produce the Video
steps.console: Record voiceover with ElevenLabs or direct to camera. Capture B-roll. Edit in CapCut — cut every pause over 1.5 seconds.

steps.name: Optimise and Publish
steps.console: Write the SEO package: title under 60 characters, keyword-first description, 15 tags mixing broad and long-tail. Publish. First 48 hours are critical.

steps.name: Repurpose and Distribute
steps.console: Extract the best 60 seconds for a Short. Write an 8-tweet thread. Draft a LinkedIn post. One video becomes five assets.

steps.name: Define the Premise
steps.console: Write one sentence: what does this book argue or teach, and for whom? If you cannot summarise it in one sentence, the book is not ready to write.

steps.name: Build the Outline
steps.console: Ask Claude to generate a 10-chapter structure. Each chapter needs one core idea and one anchor story. The outline is the argument — pressure-test it before writing.

steps.name: Research and Evidence
steps.console: Use Perplexity AI to find one strong data point, one case study, and one counterargument per chapter. Build your evidence library before touching prose.

steps.name: Draft Each Chapter
steps.console: Write one chapter per session. Give Claude the chapter goal, the core argument, and a 2,500-word target. Do not edit while drafting — velocity over perfection.

steps.name: Edit and Refine
steps.console: Edit macro first: does the argument hold? Then micro: cut the 3 weakest sentences per chapter. Run a readability pass in ProWritingAid. Delete all passive voice.

steps.name: Format and Publish
steps.console: Format in Atticus for both print and ebook. Upload to Amazon KDP. Set your launch strategy: ARC readers first, then email list, then social.

steps.name: Validate the Problem
steps.console: Talk to 10 potential customers before writing a single line of code. Confirm this is a recurring pain they actively pay to solve. No validation — no build.

steps.name: Define the MVP
steps.console: Scope the minimum version that proves your core hypothesis. One job done well. Cut everything that does not answer: does this solve the problem?

steps.name: Build the Product
steps.console: Use Cursor and Supabase to ship in 4–6 weeks, not 6 months. Hard-code what you can fake. The goal is your first real user, not a perfect product.

steps.name: Test with Real Users
steps.console: Get 10 beta users before any marketing. Watch them use the product without explaining anything. What confuses them is your product roadmap.

steps.name: Launch Publicly
steps.console: Ship to Product Hunt, post build-in-public on X, and email your waitlist. A launch reveals what to fix faster than months of internal testing.

steps.name: Scale What Works
steps.console: Fix retention before doubling acquisition. Identify your one growth channel. Review five metrics every Monday: activation, retention, revenue, churn, NPS.

steps.name: Map the Manual Process
steps.console: Document every step of the current workflow before touching any tool. List each click, decision, and handoff. Never automate a broken process.

steps.name: Identify the Trigger
steps.console: Define the exact event that starts this process: a new email, a form submission, a scheduled time, or a data change. No trigger — no automation.

steps.name: Choose the Platform
steps.console: Match the tool to the complexity. Zapier for speed. Make.com for visual logic. n8n for self-hosted. Custom Python when no-code cannot do the job.

steps.name: Build the Flow
steps.console: Construct one module at a time. Test each step in isolation before connecting the full chain. Add a filter for every decision point a human currently makes.

steps.name: Test on Real Data
steps.console: Run 10 real test cases before enabling on production data. Verify each module output is exactly correct. Define your rollback plan before going live.

steps.name: Deploy and Monitor
steps.console: Enable live. Set failure alerts to Slack or email within 60 seconds of any error. Review run logs every day for the first week. Document everything.

steps.name: Define the AI Task
steps.console: Write the exact task the AI performs in one sentence: input, transformation, output. If you cannot write it in one sentence, the product is not scoped tightly enough.

steps.name: Engineer the Prompt
steps.console: The prompt is the product. Write the full system prompt: role, task, output format, edge case handling, and hard rules. Test it on 20 inputs before building any UI.

steps.name: Build the Integration
steps.console: Connect the prompt to a minimal interface using the Anthropic or OpenAI SDK. A text input, an API call, and a result display is enough to test your core hypothesis.

steps.name: Evaluate Output Quality
steps.console: Build a 20-case eval set: 10 standard, 5 edge cases, 5 adversarial inputs. Score each output on your quality criteria. What you measure, you can systematically improve.

steps.name: Add Billing and Ship
steps.console: Integrate Stripe before scaling. Model options: freemium, usage-based, or subscription tiers. Calculate your cost per user at 100, 1,000, and 10,000 requests per month.

steps.name: Iterate from Feedback
steps.console: Add thumbs up / down ratings to every output. Low-rated outputs reveal prompt weaknesses. Version your prompts. A/B test improvements without disrupting all users.
